title: Universal Green-Chemistry Workflow for RNA Extraction and Integrity Analysis
author: Abdulhadi Albaser

steps.title: Harvest Cells
steps.instruction: Harvest Cells: Centrifuge 500 µL of culture (OD₆₀₀ 0.8–1.0) at 7,000 rpm for 5 mins at room temperature. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspension
steps.instruction: Resuspension: Resuspend the pellet in 200 µL of autoclaved distilled water.

steps.title: Lysis
steps.instruction: Lysis: Add 300 µL of acidic lysis buffer (pH 5.0). Mix gently by pipetting. Buffer Composition: 69.4 mM SDS, 68 mM sodium citrate dihydrate, 132 mM anhydrous citric acid, and 10 mM EDTA.

steps.title: Cell Disruption (Boiling Step)
steps.instruction: Cell Disruption (Boiling Step): Incubate the mixture at 100°C for 2 minutes. Note: This step is crucial for the robust cell walls of Gram-positive bacteria and yeast.

steps.title: Initial Precipitation
steps.instruction: Initial Precipitation: Add 150 µL of precipitation buffer (4 M NaCl with citrate/citric acid). Invert 10 times.

steps.title: Cooling
steps.instruction: Cooling: Incubate on ice for 7 minutes, then centrifuge at 16,000 xg for 3 mins.

steps.title: Selective RNA Isolation
steps.instruction: Selective RNA Isolation: Transfer supernatant to a new tube containing 0.5 M Calcium Chloride (CaCl₂). Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes. Innovation: CaCl₂ precipitates RNA while keeping DNA and proteins in solution.

steps.title: Pelleting
steps.instruction: Pelleting: Centrifuge at 16,000 xg for 5 mins. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Wash 6 Dry
steps.instruction: Wash 6 Dry: Wash the pellet with ice-cold 70% ethanol, centrifuge for 3 mins, and air dry for 10–20 mins.

steps.title: Resuspension
steps.instruction: Resuspension: Dissolve the dried RNA in 50 µL of autoclaved distilled water.

steps.title: Prepare Gel
steps.instruction: Prepare Gel: Dissolve 0.8%–1.0% agarose in 5 mM Borax solution (sodium tetraborate decahydrate).

steps.title: Add Stain
steps.instruction: Add Stain: Add GelRed or Ethidium Bromide to the melted agarose before casting.

steps.title: Buffer
steps.instruction: Buffer: Use the same 5 mM Borax solution as the running buffer.

steps.title: Borax acts as an "apparent denaturant,"...
steps.instruction: Borax acts as an "apparent denaturant," resolving RNA structures without pre-heating.

steps.title: Sample Preparation
steps.instruction: Sample Preparation: Mix 20 µL of RNA with 2 µL of 10X loading buffer. Pro-Tip: Use a colorless loading buffer (no Bromophenol Blue) for the sharpest visualization of small 5S rRNA bands.

steps.title: Loading
steps.instruction: Loading: Load samples directly onto the gel. No formaldehyde pre-treatment or cooling on ice is required.

steps.title: Electrophoresis
steps.instruction: Electrophoresis: Run at 120 V for 25 minutes. Comparison: This is significantly faster than standard TAE gels which require 60 minutes.

steps.title: Visualization and Image Acquisition
steps.instruction: Visualization and Image Acquisition

steps.title: Image Capture
steps.instruction: Image Capture: After electrophoresis, place the gel on a UV transilluminator (302 nm or 312 nm) or a blue light imaging system.

steps.title: Band Identification
steps.instruction: Band Identification: For Bacteria (GP & GN): Look for two prominent, sharp bands. The upper band is the 23S rRNA (~2.9 kb) and the lower band is the 16S rRNA (~1.5 kb). For Yeast (Eukaryotic): Look for the 28S rRNA (upper band, ~3.8–4.8 kb) and the 18S rRNA (lower band, ~1.9–2.0 kb). Small RNA: A smaller, fainter band near the bottom of the gel represents the 5S rRNA and tRNAs.

steps.title: Integrity Assessment
steps.instruction: Integrity Assessment: Ratio: In high-quality, intact RNA, the intensity of the upper band (23S/28S) should be approximately twice the intensity of the lower band (16S/18S). Degradation: A smear of low-molecular-weight fragments or the disappearance of the 23S/28S band indicates RNA degradation. DNA Contamination: High-molecular-weight bands near the wells indicate genomic DNA (gDNA) contamination.
